channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Nido shareholders approve CEO incentive plan linked to quality and compliance outcomes
item.description: Nido Education shareholders have approved a revised executive incentive plan that links a significant portion of Chief Executive Officer Adam Lai's long-term remuneration to quality ratings, compliance performance and safety outcomes across the provider's early learning network.
item.content:encoded: Nido Education shareholders have approved a revised executive incentive plan that links a significant portion of Chief Executive Officer Adam Lai's long-term remuneration to quality ratings, compliance performance and safety outcomes across the provider's early learning network.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: UK regulator steps up unannounced visits and the parallels with Australia are hard to ignore
item.description: Ofsted's decision to increase unannounced visits to early years settings in England comes at a time when Australia's own early childhood education and care (ECEC) regulatory environment is facing heightened scrutiny around quality, compliance and child safety.
item.content:encoded: Ofsted's decision to increase unannounced visits to early years settings in England comes at a time when Australia's own early childhood education and care (ECEC) regulatory environment is facing heightened scrutiny around quality, compliance and child safety.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: ECEC awards finalists highlight excellence across large and small providers
item.description: The announcement of this year's Excellence in Early Childhood Education and Care (ECEC) Awards finalists has revealed an inspiring picture of quality, innovation and dedication across the sector, with organisations of all sizes earning recognition for their contribution to children, families and communities.
item.content:encoded: The announcement of this year's Excellence in Early Childhood Education and Care (ECEC) Awards finalists has revealed an inspiring picture of quality, innovation and dedication across the sector, with organisations of all sizes earning recognition for their contribution to children, families and communities.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: What a potential NSW community preschool funding boost could mean for early learning
item.description: The NSW Government has signalled that increased funding for community preschools may be on the horizon, with the Independent Education Union of Australia (IEU) NSW/ACT Branch advising that it has been informed that a funding announcement is likely in the coming weeks.
item.content:encoded: The NSW Government has signalled that increased funding for community preschools may be on the horizon, with the Independent Education Union of Australia (IEU) NSW/ACT Branch advising that it has been informed that a funding announcement is likely in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: New national project to strengthen equity in early learning underway
item.description: A new national project supported by the Investment Dialogue for Australia's Children (IDAC) is set to examine how Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) system can better respond to the diverse needs of children, families and communities.
item.content:encoded: A new national project supported by the Investment Dialogue for Australia's Children (IDAC) is set to examine how Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) system can better respond to the diverse needs of children, families and communities.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: NSW introduces enhancements to Assessment and Rating from 1 July 2026
item.description: NSW early childhood education and care (ECEC) services will see significant changes to the Assessment and Rating (A&R) process from 1 July 2026, with the NSW Department of Education introducing a strengthened, year-round approach to continuous improvement, service readiness and regulatory assurance.
item.content:encoded: NSW early childhood education and care (ECEC) services will see significant changes to the Assessment and Rating (A&R) process from 1 July 2026, with the NSW Department of Education introducing a strengthened, year-round approach to continuous improvement, service readiness and regulatory assurance.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: New national RPL resources released to help educators and employers identify quality training - The Sector
item.description: New national RPL resources released to help educators and employers identify quality training - The Sector
item.content:encoded: New national RPL resources released to help educators and employers identify quality training - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Shaping Bright Futures: WorkinEarlyLearning launches to tackle the ECEC Sector's workforce crisis - The Sector
item.description: Shaping Bright Futures: WorkinEarlyLearning launches to tackle the ECEC Sector's workforce crisis - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Shaping Bright Futures: WorkinEarlyLearning launches to tackle the ECEC Sector's workforce crisis - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Thriving Kids must be built with Aboriginal communities to succeed
item.description: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.content:encoded: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Marlee Silva – National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador
item.description: Marlee Silva is the National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador! Find out more in the Ambassador Video!
item.content:encoded: Marlee Silva is the National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador! Find out more in the Ambassador Video!

item.dc:creator: SMH.com.au

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.description: SNAICC responds to Worker Retention Payment extension, NT and Queensland child protection scrutiny, Aboriginal Family-Led Decision Making and Children’s Day.

item.content:encoded: Worker Retention Payment extended to 2028: what early childhood providers need to know - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Investing in educators means investing in children
item.description: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.content:encoded: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
